claim: On-policy RL (where models generate and learn from their own outputs) is more generalizable than off-policy approaches (imitation learning from other models' trajectories)
actionable: When training LLM agents, structure training as on-policy: generate model outputs, apply reward signal to its own generations, then train on those outputs rather than copying others' trajectories
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Abandoning specialized symbolic systems (like AlphaProof's Lean verifier) in favor of end-to-end LLMs with embedded capabilities (like verifying Lean proofs via chain-of-thought) is the right path toward AGI
actionable: Instead of building separate symbolic systems or tool integrations, focus on scaling end-to-end LLMs to encode specialized knowledge directly in parameters through post-training
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: IMO Gold scoring is determined by percentile ranking of human participants, not a fixed threshold, making real-time uncertainty during live competitions about whether the gold standard will be met
actionable: When benchmarking against competitions with adaptive thresholds, track percentile-based scoring dynamics rather than assuming fixed cutoffs
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: The IMO Gold effort's success relied on distributed collaboration across time zones (Singapore, London, Mountain View) with ad-hoc handoffs between captains, not formal coordination protocols
actionable: For distributed high-stakes projects, establish clear async handoff patterns and status checkpoints rather than synchronous coordination; accept that team members will overlap for critical phases
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Learning rate adjustments (how fast you update beliefs when wrong) apply to human learning: one counter-example should trigger large belief updates (50-100%) not small Bayesian updates (2%), invalidating prior 10 years of experience
actionable: When evidence contradicts your established worldview, deliberately increase your 'learning rate' and update aggressively on the counter-example rather than treating it as noise in your prior distribution
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Self-consistency in reasoning involves multiple parallel reasoning passes from the same model, evaluated by an LLM judge assessing internal consistency and chain-of-thought validity, not majority voting
actionable: When implementing self-consistency for complex reasoning, use an LLM judge to evaluate consistency of reasoning traces across multiple generations rather than simple majority voting
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Pokémon Crystal is a valuable long-horizon agentic reasoning benchmark because it requires multi-step planning, strategy, and environment understanding without heavy domain-specific optimization
actionable: Use Pokémon Crystal as a benchmark for testing long-horizon planning and multi-step reasoning capabilities; it resists benchmark gaming because players don't typically optimize explicitly for it
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Modern LLM-based reasoning is essentially improved post-training and RL (not just discrete chain-of-thought decoding), including both visible token reasoning and potentially latent space reasoning without explicit token output
actionable: When discussing reasoning capabilities, recognize that reasoning encompasses post-training techniques (RL, refinement) not just chain-of-thought prompting; latent reasoning may be computationally equivalent to token-based reasoning
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: AI coding (agentic code generation and debugging) has reached utility threshold where complex debugging tasks can be faster to delegate to the model than manually investigating, especially for bug fixes with unclear root causes
actionable: For exploratory debugging of unfamiliar code issues, use AI coding agents first; they can often identify and fix bugs faster than manual investigation, especially when root cause is unclear
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Transformer self-attention is unlikely to be fully replaced within the gradient descent + backprop paradigm; variations (local/global attention, efficiency optimizations) remain attention-based
actionable: When evaluating alternative architectures, assess whether they truly replace attention or merely optimize it; fundamental paradigm shifts (away from backprop) may be required for architectural replacements
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Data efficiency improvements in LLMs could come from spending more compute per token (not just having more tokens) or fundamentally different learning algorithms, similar to how humans learn efficiently from far less data
actionable: Investigate data efficiency along two axes: (1) allocate more compute to each training token, (2) explore alternative learning algorithms that don't require transformer scaling patterns
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Generative retrieval (DSI/semantic IDs) transforms ranking/retrieval from dense vector matching to predicting semantic document identifiers, enabling LLMs to handle unusual query intents that BM25 cannot
actionable: For recommendation and retrieval tasks, explore semantic token IDs as an alternative to vector similarity matching; this approach generalizes better to out-of-distribution queries
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: The retrieval/recommendation domain feels unintuitive compared to other ML domains (cause-effect feedback is disconnected), making it harder to develop intuition despite being a critical business problem
actionable: If working on retrieval/ranking systems, recognize that intuition from standard ML won't transfer; invest in online evaluation infrastructure and rapid iteration to build domain intuition
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Strong research taste (identifying novel, independently valuable directions) can be demonstrated by grad students with limited resources; this is more predictive of capability than credentials or prior domain knowledge
actionable: When hiring or recruiting researchers, prioritize evidence of research taste (tasteful, novel work on relevant problems) over traditional credentials; guide candidates to explore 5 promising directions independently
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: End-to-end AI system design (no symbolic subsystems) works for mathematical reasoning tasks; the model encodes domain knowledge (like Lean verification) through parameters rather than explicit tool integration
actionable: For AI systems that need specialized domain reasoning, avoid separate tool integrations; instead invest in scaling end-to-end LLM training to embed the required reasoning capabilities directly
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Physical health (weight loss, HRV, resting heart rate improvements) directly correlates with research productivity and intellectual capacity; a 23kg weight loss over 1.5 years improved HRV 2x and reduced resting heart rate 30 BPM
actionable: Treat physical health optimization (sleep, exercise, nutrition) as a research productivity multiplier; track HRV and resting heart rate as proxies for cognitive capacity
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: RL is now the primary modeling tool for frontier LLM work; architectural research (pre-LLM era focus) has shifted to post-training and reinforcement learning as the main lever for capability improvements
actionable: If entering LLM research now, prioritize deep RL knowledge over architecture exploration; post-training optimization is where capability gains are being unlocked
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Ideas and fundamental research still matter significantly even in the age of scaling; blindly scaling inferior architectures (like MLPs without attention) with massive compute won't work
actionable: Reject the pure scaling narrative; continue to invest in novel ideas and architecture/training paradigm innovations, as they compound with scale
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: The gap between closed-lab capabilities and open-source model availability is increasing; proprietary training tricks and post-training methods compounds researcher advantage
actionable: If building open-source research, focus on novel paradigms or data rather than trying to match closed-lab post-training techniques; expect the gap to widen
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay

claim: Geographic location for AI research matters less for collaboration (all work is inherently distributed across time zones) but matters for talent density, local culture/mental space, and time zone coverage
actionable: When establishing research teams, prioritize 24-hour time zone coverage and talent density over single-location optimization; cultural diversity can help maintain intellectual independence
source_title: Captaining IMO Gold, Deep Think, On-Policy RL, Feeling the AGI in Singapore — Yi Tay
